transcript.clean_text: at gcn we are very lucky to get to show you guys some of the most exclusive Pro cycling Tech that's on show at races like the Jiro but some of it is a little harder to spot so here's a quick look at some of the more subtle and less noticeable bits of tech that we spotted at the 2017 jro dalala stage one of the 2017 jro was a bit of a surprise Victory it's safe to say for Lucas pelberg of borans looked like he was going to go and lead out his teammate s Bennett which he did so admirably that he actually got a huge gap on the bunch and went on to solo and take the victory it was actually very very nice to see really cool stage win and to celebrate his stage win his Bor hands girl team got the pink bartape they've got the pink bottle cages and the mechanics are actually rocking pink socks as well and the prize for I think the most different position on the bike here at the ziral goes to Zas bikov of the aana team he is clearly a very tall chap he's got an extender on the head tube of his argon 18 bike he's then got 20 cm of spacer and he's got the stem angled up just to get his desired position and the head tube on the bike is massive to start with and there is safe to say a lot of seat post sticking out too shows that not every pro cyclist goes for a Super Slam position what is important is getting the position that fits and works for you French brand Cora actually came out with a bunch of new wheels that was signed off by the UCI at the start of 2017 these ones on this Asana team bike are the 47 MCC s+ Wheels theyve got a couple of stickers on the wheel that say magnet so I'm guessing that might mean they have a built-in magnet into the wheels for Speedos and stuff like that and they are very minimalistic this one has just 12 carbon spokes in the front wheel cool I quite the dimension data team are sponsored by ceramic speed we can see they've got ceramic speed bottom bracket bearings in across the board but the only Rider with the ceramic speed rear met cage and jockey wheels that we can see is Nathan H those are huge the theory is that the the bigger jockey Wheels actually increase efficiency and thus help you to move forward faster but they definitely look very very different to the Shimano J race ones and I think very cool most of the custom paint jobs that you see on ProBox is pretty of it's pretty out there but this one is very subtle and very cool this is Daniel teut erran Champion sevell I just I really like just how subtle it is I don't like the colors behind me is Adam yates's Scott addict and if you look very closely at the bottom bracket cap or the crank cap that is quite a lot different from the standard dur race ones it's got a bolt through it and those are actually custom made for the team and that is where they put in the lead fishing weights I believe to weigh the bikes down when they're below the UCI weight limit we're at the Ziro ahead of one of the hilliest stages early on in the race so it makes sense they're going to make his bike as light as possible also because Yates is one of perhaps like the slightly shorter riders in the pelaton it means his bike smaller and therefore lighter it's always quite cool to see subtle bits of tech like that really like it uh quite an interesting one here and also quite a difficult one to spot is that the a does a team spare bikes are actually pretty well fully equipped with Shimano altegra save the dur race chain sets they've got altegra shifters mechs and brakes on a tire BR That You Don't See too often in the world tour Pelon is TFO but they're here with the gazprom team at the zero these are pretty interesting they're quite narrow by current standards they're 23 not 25 and also they're pretty hard so they've got a recommended pressure of 8 to 15 bar which is 115 to 220 PSI which again I think many riders are probably running 100 to 110 maybe maximum and they actually probably are that hard very accurate flick test yeah 115 possibly maybe more although this isn't strictly Bike Tech the UCI commissaires are actually here already before the start just checking over every team's bikes with their tablets or iPads I don't know which one it is to check for any signs of hidden Motors it's quite cool to see they're still doing this at every race because of course the last thing that anyone wants to happen is to have one of those mechanical doping positives again a pretty cool thing that the uh Canon del draak team do is that most of their Riders ride 130 BCD cranks which means they can't fit the compact chain Rings like 34s or 36 that we might be used to seeing so many of them ride 38 tooth inner Rings which is the smallest chaining that you can get on a30 BCD which was the previous Shimano standard up until a few years ago and it just means that they've got that slightly gear for the slightly steeper Hills here at the zerro we'd really like to hear the really subtle ways that you make your bike go faster too so let us know your Tech hacks down in the comments if you've enjoyed this video hit the thumbs up button and share it with your friends too to subscribe to gcn and to get more great content like this click on our logo which is on screen right now to see another tech video from the 2017 jro click right there and to see a Pro bike from the 2017 jro just there
